medical_history.history_of_present_illness: Patient with a history of dementia, HTN, and hypothyroidism presents as a transfer from an outside hospital for a right distal humerus fracture after tripping and falling while walking with a walker. She has abrasions to her head and her extremities. Initial evaluation showed her right humerus x-ray indicates an angulated, displaced distal humerus fracture. Negative findings on head and C-spine CTs, bilateral knee x-rays, CXR, and pelvis x-ray. At the time, she denies any pain, numbness, weakness, headache, neck pain, back pain, chest pain, shortness of breath, or abdominal pain. Not on any anticoagulation.

physical_examination.general_examination: Well-appearing, breathing comfortably. General vitals not specified.
physical_examination.specialty_examination: RUE in cast. Able to move wrist and fingers. SILT distally. WWP.
